name: Wave Interference: Superposition Principle
description: Explore the principle of superposition. When two waves occupy the same space, their displacements add together. This simulation visualizes the interference of two sine waves: $y_1 = A_1 \sin(k_1x - \omega_1t)$ and $y_2 = A_2 \sin(k_2x - \omega_2t)$. Observe how constructive and destructive interference patterns emerge.
renderingCode: class ScienceSimulation { constructor(container, initialInputs) { this.container = container; this.canvas = document.createElement('canvas'); this.container.appendChild(this.canvas); this.ctx = this.canvas.getContext('2d'); this.time = 0; this.animate(); } animate() { this.time += 0.05; this.render({}, -1); requestAnimationFrame(() => this.animate()); } getSteps(inputs) { return [{ title: "The Waves", description: "Two individual waves are generated with frequencies $f_1 = " + inputs.freq1 + "$ Hz and $f_2 = " + inputs.freq2 + "$ Hz." }, { title: "Superposition", description: "The resultant wave is the sum of the two: $y_{total} = y_1 + y_2$. Where crests meet, we see constructive interference. Where a crest meets a trough, destructive interference occurs." }]; } render(inputs, stepIndex) { const w = this.canvas.width = this.container.clientWidth; const h = this.canvas.height = 300; const ctx = this.ctx; ctx.clearRect(0, 0, w, h); const cx = w / 2; const cy = h / 2; const f1 = parseFloat(inputs.freq1 || 2); const f2 = parseFloat(inputs.freq2 || 3); const a1 = parseFloat(inputs.amp1 || 1); const a2 = parseFloat(inputs.amp2 || 1); ctx.beginPath(); ctx.strokeStyle = '#60a5fa'; ctx.lineWidth = 2; for(let x = 0; x < w; x++) { const y = cy + Math.sin(x * 0.05 + this.time * f1) * (a1 * 20); if(x === 0) ctx.moveTo(x, y); else ctx.lineTo(x, y); } ctx.stroke(); ctx.beginPath(); ctx.strokeStyle = '#f87171'; for(let x = 0; x < w; x++) { const y = cy + Math.sin(x * 0.05 + this.time * f2) * (a2 * 20); if(x === 0) ctx.moveTo(x, y); else ctx.lineTo(x, y); } ctx.stroke(); ctx.beginPath(); ctx.strokeStyle = '#ffffff'; ctx.lineWidth = 4; for(let x = 0; x < w; x++) { const y1 = Math.sin(x * 0.05 + this.time * f1) * (a1 * 20); const y2 = Math.sin(x * 0.05 + this.time * f2) * (a2 * 20); const y = cy + (y1 + y2); if(x === 0) ctx.moveTo(x, y); else ctx.lineTo(x, y); } ctx.stroke(); ctx.fillStyle = "white"; ctx.fillText("Wave 1 (Blue)", 10, 20); ctx.fillText("Wave 2 (Red)", 10, 40); ctx.fillText("Resultant (White)", 10, 60); } }
